title: Acwa wins desalination awards at Madrid ceremony
content:
Acwa Power swept two major awards at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, winning Desalination Company of the Year and Desalination Plant of the Year honors. The company manages 9.7 million m³/day of water desalination capacity, positioning it as the world's largest private water desalination company.

The awards, organized by Global Water Intelligence since 2006, recognize excellence across 15 categories in the international water sector. Acwa's wins mark its second time receiving these honors.

Dr. Tariq Nada, Head of Technical Services for Global Water at Acwa, attributed the recognition to engineers, operators and partners across the company's ecosystem. "We are proud to provide a significant share of Saudi Arabia's desalinated water capacity," Nada said in a statement.

Yasir Mahmoud, Executive Vice President of Global Water, said the dual awards validated Acwa's operational model. "Being named Desalination Company of the Year, alongside Desalination Plant of the Year, is the clearest signal yet that our model works — lower energy intensity, higher availability and smarter financing," Mahmoud said.

The company's flagship Rabigh 3 Independent Water Project, a 600,000 m³/day seawater reverse osmosis facility serving the Makkah region, has maintained availability above 99%. The plant recently completed a refinancing aligned with IFC Blue Finance Guidelines.

Acwa operates 109 assets across operation, advanced development and construction phases in its wider portfolio. The company also positions itself as a leader in renewable energy and has moved into green hydrogen production at scale.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-recognized-as-a-global-leader-in-water-at-the-2026-global-water-awards-in-madrid/

title: Acwa Power, BCDA to Build 500-MW Solar-Storage Project in Philippines
content:
Acwa Power Philippines signed a contract of lease with the Bases Conversion and Development Authority for a 500-hectare site in New Clark City to develop an integrated solar photovoltaic and battery energy storage system facility.

The project represents an initial investment of at least USD 400,000 per megawatt, with capacity of up to 500 MW upon full build-out, subject to final design, regulatory approvals, and future expansion phases. The facility will power data centers and advanced industries within the Philippines' flagship smart city.

Acwa Power, a Saudi Arabia-based subsidiary of Acwa Power Company, is a major player in desalination and energy transition projects. The investment was secured under the administration of President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. as part of the country's sustainable economic growth and energy security objectives.

Finance Secretary Frederick D. Go said the project "lays the foundation upon which semiconductors, advanced manufacturing, and AI-driven industries can be built at utility scale."

Acwa Power Chief Executive Officer Dr. Samir J Serhan said the partnership reflected growing investor confidence in the Philippines for large-scale clean infrastructure. "New Clark City reflects the kind of integrated, future-ready development that global energy investors are looking for," Serhan stated.

Salman M. Baray, Country General Manager of Acwa Philippines, said the scale of the lease within the special economic zone would allow the company to build an integrated rather than incremental project.

The development is one of the key investment commitments secured by the Philippine government to support the country's broader economic ambitions through reliable renewable energy.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-bcda-advance-philippines-clean-energy-transition-with-500-mw-solar-plus-storage-project-in-new-clark-city/

title: Acwa, Emerging Power to develop 5,000 MW clean energy platform in Philippines by 2030
content:
Acwa Power Philippines and Emerging Power Inc. signed a joint development agreement to pursue up to 5,000 MW of renewable energy and gas-fired generation capacity across the Philippines, the companies announced on June 2.

The partnership targets national-scale renewable and gas-fired generation development, with an initial focus on bidding jointly into the Philippines' Green Energy Auction Program, or GEAP. Emerging Power Inc. is the clean energy arm of Nickel Asia Corporation, one of Southeast Asia's largest lateritic nickel ore producers.

Acwa, a Saudi-listed company, operates as a major private water desalination firm and renewable energy developer. The two companies plan to identify and co-develop new power projects nationwide under the agreement.

"The Philippines has set one of the most ambitious clean energy trajectories in Southeast Asia, and reaching it requires partners who can move at scale and at pace," Acwa Chief Executive Officer Samir J Serhan said in a statement.

Emerging Power Chairman and CEO Martin Antonio G. Zamora said the partnership would accelerate the nation's transition to a low-carbon economy. "Together, we are building scalable, reliable, and sustainable power infrastructure that will energize Filipino communities, drive economic growth, and safeguard our generations to come," he said.

Salman M. Baray, country general manager of Acwa Philippines, said the collaboration combines local execution capability with global development experience. He described GEAP participation as an initial test, with a longer pipeline of projects to be developed jointly.

Source: https://acwapower.com/en/media-center/latest-news/acwa-and-emerging-power-inc-sign-joint-development-agreement-jda-to-build-up-to-5-000-mw-clean-energy-platform-by-2030-in-the-philippines/

title: EDF, Masdar Sign 15-Year PPA for California Solar-Storage Project
content:
EDF power solutions North America and Masdar have signed 15-year power purchase agreements covering output from the BigBeau Solar+Storage Project in Kern County, California. The facility consists of a 128 megawatt solar plant and a 40 MW/160 MWh battery energy storage system that has operated since December 2022. Electricity delivery to Southern California Edison began February 1, 2026, under the new agreements.

Dustin Priemer, Director Asset Management Masdar Americas, said the agreement reflects the company's focus on scaling reliable, utility-scale clean power in the United States. "We are appreciative of our growing partnership with Southern California Edison and our shared commitment to investing in new generation capacity to meet growing energy demand in California," Priemer said.

Jacqueline de Fresart, Associate Director of Origination and Power Marketing at EDF power solutions North America, added that the company is pleased to support SCE's clean energy goals. "We are excited to partner with SCE again and look forward to more opportunities together," de Fresart said.

BigBeau represents one of seven projects that Masdar and EDF power solutions agreed to develop together, with combined capacity of 1.1 gigawatts. EDF power solutions has developed 26 gigawatts of wind, solar and storage projects across North America over 35 years. Masdar, active in the United States since 2019, has invested several billion dollars and plans to develop a portfolio of up to 25 GW within the next decade.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/edf-power-solutions-north-america-and-masdar-sign-agreements-for-solar-and-energy-storage-project

title: RWE, Masdar get green light for 3GW Dogger Bank South offshore wind
content:
RWE and Masdar received planning permission for two offshore wind farms with a combined capacity of 3 gigawatts off England's northeast coast, the UK Energy Secretary confirmed on May 14, 2026.

Lord Whitehead, on behalf of the Energy Secretary, approved the Development Consent Order for Dogger Bank South East and Dogger Bank South West, each rated at 1.5GW. The projects are located more than 100 kilometers offshore and are being developed as a joint venture between RWE, which holds a 51% stake, and Masdar with 49%.

The wind farms are expected to generate enough electricity annually to power approximately three million UK homes. Both projects secured Contracts for Difference under the UK Government's Allocation Round 7 in January 2026.

RWE and Masdar submitted the Development Consent Order application in June 2024, including over 1,000 documents reviewed by the Planning Inspectorate. Ten online examination hearings were held during the assessment process.

The companies will now conduct detailed final design work and procurement decisions, targeting a final investment decision in 2027. The offshore installations have the potential to support businesses and communities in Yorkshire and the Humber region.

More information about the projects is available at doggerbanksouth.co.uk.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/rwe-and-masdar-receive-planning-permission-for-3gw-dogger-bank-south-offshore-wind-farm-projects

title: RWE secures 6.9 GW of UK offshore wind contracts at £91.20/MWh
content:
RWE has secured Contracts for Difference for 6.9 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity across five projects in the UK government's Allocation Round 7 auction, at a strike price of £91.20 per megawatt hour in 2024 prices, inflation-indexed.

The German energy company won 20-year contracts for Norfolk Vanguard East and West, two Dogger Bank South projects in the British North Sea, and Awel y Môr in the Irish Sea, the UK Department for Energy Security and Net Zero confirmed on Tuesday.

RWE will develop Norfolk Vanguard East and West jointly with KKR, the global investment firm, which will acquire 50% equity stakes in each project. Both companies will jointly develop, construct and operate the wind farms. The transaction is expected to close in summer 2026, subject to customary approvals.

"We are delighted to have been successful in securing long-term offtake contracts for five projects in AR7," RWE Chief Executive Markus Krebber said. "By combining KKR's investment know-how in large-scale, complex infrastructure projects with RWE's extensive offshore wind expertise, we are well positioned to jointly realise these major projects."

For the Dogger Bank South projects, RWE partners with Masdar, the Abu Dhabi renewable energy company. At Awel y Môr, RWE collaborates with Stadtwerke München and Siemens.

The awards represent a significant milestone for RWE's UK offshore wind development pipeline as the energy transition accelerates across Britain.

Source: https://uk.rwe.com/press-and-news/2026-01-14-rwe-secures-contracts-for-difference-for-6-9-gigawatts-of-offshore-wind-capacity/

title: EWEC, Masdar sign framework to scale 30GW solar and 8GW battery storage in UAE
content:
EWEC and Masdar signed a strategic collaboration framework agreement to accelerate deployment of more than 30GW of solar photovoltaic capacity and over 8GW of battery storage across the United Arab Emirates.

The agreement, signed by Ahmed Ali Alshamsi, chief executive of EWEC, and Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, chief executive of Masdar, establishes a roadmap for utility-scale renewable energy project deployment to diversify the UAE's energy mix and strengthen supply security. The framework aims to support Abu Dhabi's goal of meeting 60% of total energy demand from renewable and clean sources by 2035.

EWEC, which handles integrated planning, purchasing, supply and system dispatch services for water and electricity across the UAE, said the collaboration will streamline Masdar's participation in its renewable energy procurement processes from early-stage development through financial close. The agreement preserves transparency and competition in EWEC's bidding procedures, the company said.

The two entities have previously partnered on major projects including Al Dhafra Solar PV, Al Ajban Solar PV, Khazna Solar PV, and a gigascale round-the-clock solar and battery storage facility.

Alshamsi said the framework reflects EWEC's commitment to optimising processes to reduce costs and improve operations. He added the collaboration ensures the company remains on track to meet decarbonisation targets while powering local industrial growth and maximising in-country value through development of Emirati talent and expertise.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/wec-and-masdar-forge-strategic-framework-to-scale-renewable-energy

title: Masdar, Montenegro utility form 50/50 renewable energy venture
content:
Abu Dhabi-based Masdar and Montenegro's national power utility EPCG have agreed to establish a 50/50 joint venture to develop large-scale renewable energy projects across the country.

The partnership, signed at the Adria Future Summit, will deploy and operate clean energy projects including solar photovoltaic, wind, hydropower, pumped hydro energy storage, stand-alone battery energy storage systems and hybrid solutions. The joint venture will be headquartered in Nikšić.

Masdar CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, EPCG President Milutin Đukanović and EPCG CEO Zdravko Dragaš signed the agreement in the presence of Montenegro's Minister of Energy and Mining Admir Šahmanović and the United Arab Emirates Ambassador to Montenegro.

The collaboration aims to support Montenegro's domestic energy needs while enabling the export of renewable electricity to the Western Balkans and Southern Europe, including through an existing sub-sea interconnection with Italy.

The joint venture will combine EPCG's position as Montenegro's leading energy producer with Masdar's global expertise in renewable energy development to enhance the country's energy security and accelerate its energy transition.

Minister Šahmanović said the partnership represents a turning point for Montenegro's energy future. "By joining forces, Masdar and EPCG bring together global knowledge, strong national capacity and a shared ambition to speed up the transition towards clean energy," he said in a statement.

The agreement builds on Masdar's existing presence in Montenegro and complements its expansion across the wider region.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/masdar-and-epcg-to-form-joint-venture

title: ScottishPower, Masdar install UK's largest offshore wind blades
content:
ScottishPower and Masdar have installed the first turbine at their East Anglia THREE offshore windfarm with 115-metre blades, the largest manufactured and deployed in the UK.

The 1.4 GW project off the Suffolk coast marks the first time UK offshore wind turbines feature blades of this length. All 285 blades for the project's 95 turbines are manufactured at Siemens Gamesa's factory in Hull.

The 14MW Siemens Gamesa turbines stand approximately 262 metres tall with a rotor diameter of 236 metres. A single turbine revolution produces enough electricity to power a UK home for more than four days, according to the developers.

When complete, East Anglia THREE will generate sufficient clean power for more than 1.3 million homes. The £4 billion project will rank among the world's largest offshore windfarms.

Charlie Jordan, CEO of ScottishPower Renewables, said the installation represents "a defining moment" and "a UK industry first." He added that the project will invest billions in UK and global supply chains, support thousands of construction jobs, and create more than 100 long-term roles in the East of England.

Husain Al Meer, Director of Global Offshore Wind at Masdar, described the turbine installation as "a milestone moment for the UK's offshore wind sector."

East Anglia THREE is a joint venture between ScottishPower, owned by Iberdrola, and the Abu Dhabi-based renewable energy company Masdar. The project is expected to enhance UK energy security through increased clean power generation.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/first-turbine-installed-at-east-anglia-three

title: WiSER platform trains 150+ women as sustainability leaders globally
content:
WiSER, a global platform established in 2015 by Masdar and the Zayed Sustainability Prize, has trained over 150 young women representing more than 30 nationalities to lead sustainable change initiatives.

The platform operates through three pillars: Education, Engagement, and Empowerment. Its flagship WiSER Pioneers Program offers women aged 25-35 a year-long curriculum combining educational workshops, mentoring, and networking with industry experts. Participants receive 540+ hours of workshop training and 565+ hours of one-on-one mentoring to develop leadership skills for the net-zero transition.

WiSER has convened over 4,000 influential figures to advance gender parity and sustainability outcomes. The organization also runs complementary programs including WiSER Cares, a cultural exchange and hands-on learning initiative focused on leadership, innovation, energy nexus, community engagement and global networking. A separate mentorship program pairs WiSER Pioneers graduates with senior industry mentors for ongoing career development.

Founded on the principle that women disproportionately affected by environmental and development challenges can drive transformative change, WiSER is guided by the United Arab Emirates' sustainability vision and the legacy of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan. The platform's annual forum convenes participants to discuss scaling a sustainable global economy. WiSER's next intake is scheduled for 1 January 2025.

Source: https://wiser.ae/

title: TotalEnergies, Masdar form $2.2bn renewable venture in Asia
content:
TotalEnergies and Masdar signed a binding agreement to establish a $2.2 billion 50/50 joint venture merging their onshore renewable activities across nine Asian countries, the companies said on April 2, 2026.

The joint venture will develop, build, own and operate onshore solar, wind and battery storage projects in Azerbaijan, Indonesia, Japan, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea and Uzbekistan. Each partner will contribute assets of comparable value to the deal.

The combined portfolio will comprise 3 GW of operational capacity and 6 GW of assets under advanced development expected to become operational by 2030. The venture will serve as the sole vehicle for both companies' onshore renewable activities in the region.

"Asia will be the main driver of global electricity demand growth this decade, and this collaboration with TotalEnergies will accelerate our progress across the continent, unlocking new opportunities to deliver the competitive, reliable energy solutions that our partners and customers need," said His Excellency Dr. Sultan Al Jaber, UAE Minister of Industry and Advanced Technology and Chairman of Masdar.

TotalEnergies highlighted the venture as bringing together two major renewable players to establish a renewable champion in Asia. The partnership combines the capital and expertise of both companies to meet accelerating electricity demand across the region at scale and speed.

The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and regulatory approvals in each country.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/totalenergies-and-masdar

title: Exus Renewables acquires 60% stake in Masdar's Portuguese wind portfolio
content:
Exus Renewables has agreed to acquire a 60% stake in a portfolio of nine wind farms in Portugal from Masdar, with the Abu Dhabi clean energy company retaining 40% ownership.

The portfolio, located in Guarda and Castelo Branco in northern and central Portugal, is undergoing repowering that will increase total capacity from 144MW to 164MW. Completion of the repowering project is expected by 2027, marking one of Portugal's first large-scale repowering initiatives.

Once fully operational, the sites will supply electricity to more than 200,000 households and avoid approximately 41.7 kilotons of CO₂ emissions annually.

Luis Adão da Fonseca, CEO at Exus Renewables, said the deal strengthens the company's operational presence across Europe and reflects its optimization-led growth strategy. "Repowering unlocks significant new renewable capacity from existing sites," he said.

Masdar CEO Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi added that the partnership underscores both companies' commitment to enhancing asset performance and long-term value. "Disciplined portfolio management and partnership are fundamental aspects of our growth strategy," he said.

The portfolio will also be enhanced through hybridization, with plans to add 110MW of solar capacity to be codeveloped by Masdar and Exus. This expansion aligns with Portugal's energy transition roadmap, which targets 10.4 GW of onshore wind capacity by 2030 and net zero emissions by 2050.

Source: https://masdar.ae/en/news/newsroom/exus-renewables-to-acquire-stake-in-masdars-portuguese-wind-portfolio

title: Masdar expands renewable energy push in Uzbekistan with $2bn investment
content:
Masdar has invested over US$2 billion in renewable energy projects across Uzbekistan since entering the country in 2018, according to a documentary in the company's Power to Change film series released on November 13.

The Abu Dhabi-based renewable energy company is driving one of Central Asia's most ambitious clean energy programs, with major developments including the 500MW Zarafshan Wind Farm and solar projects at Sherabad (457MW) and Nur Navoi (100MW).

"From the beginning, our mission was to identify how Masdar could best support Uzbekistan's transition to a green and more sustainable energy system," said Abdulla Zayed, Director of Development and Investment at Masdar.

Ilholm Jon Teshabaev, General Director of Uzbekistan Masdar, reflected on the country's energy transition in the documentary. "We know our history, our old industries and how we generated power. But the world is moving forward and so have we," he said.

The partnership is expanding further. Masdar and the Government of Uzbekistan are advancing the country's first standalone Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), designed to balance the grid, stabilize supply and ensure reliable clean power around the clock.

The projects are intended to provide secure and affordable energy to communities across Uzbekistan as the country shifts away from its industrial past toward a more sustainable energy future.

The Power to Change documentary series showcases Masdar's renewable energy initiatives globally, highlighting the role of international cooperation in accelerating the energy transition.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={AF1B6ECC-3E90-4CAF-A036-6C76456D4A95}

title: Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week draws record 60,000 attendees
content:
Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week 2026 concluded its largest edition with over 60,000 attendees, marking a 20% increase from the previous year and reinforcing the event's position as the world's largest sustainability gathering.

The week-long conference, held under the patronage of UAE President His Highness Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and hosted by Masdar, convened 15 heads of state and over 400 ministers and government officials from January 11-15. This represented more than double the number of government representatives from the previous year.

Themed 'The Nexus of Next: All Systems Go,' the event featured more than 240 dedicated sessions with over 800 speakers addressing sustainability challenges across energy, finance, food, water and nature systems. Participants included business and civil society leaders focused on accelerating real-world solutions and driving systems transformation.

The conference highlighted the UAE's position in clean energy leadership while challenging global decision-makers to rethink how major systems connect, scale and reinforce each other. The increased participation underscored growing momentum around sustainability dialogue and the transition toward integrated approaches to addressing interconnected global challenges.

Masdar, the Abu Dhabi-based clean energy company, organized the event as part of its broader commitment to advancing sustainability progress worldwide.

Source: https://masdar.ae/news/newsroom?topicID={4D4CF84D-930F-4B2C-9BE8-3584C1382126}
